name: Summit Mobility
recent_news: Family-owned business operating since 2003 serving NE Colorado mobility needs; appears focused on medical mobility equipment/supplies rather than student transport.
fit_notes: Not a school-bus/SPED operator; appears to be mobility equipment/medical supply business. Not a Summit tuck-in target.

name: VIP Medical Transportation LLC
recent_news: Veteran-owned/disabled-veteran-owned NEMT operator founded by Carey Smith (33-yr transport/law-enforcement vet); licensed/bonded by VA SCC; no school district or student-transport contracts evident.
fit_notes: Pure NEMT/medical transport micro-operator with no school district business. Not aligned with Summit's school-bus tuck-in thesis.

name: Paradise Transportation
recent_news: Very limited public footprint; SAFER lists a 'Paradise Transport LLC' but no clear linkage to Saint Paul school transport. Owner per source: Doug Nelson. Likely a micro NEMT/livery operator, not a school bus contractor.
fit_notes: Micro-operator with negligible public footprint; no evidence of school district contracts or fleet scale. Not a viable Summit tuck-in.

name: Famiks Transport Inc
recent_news: Minority-owned small business in Fayetteville NC; in operation ~24 years (founded 2002) per D&B/Buzzfile; classified as school/employee bus and special needs transport. Listed in SAM gov contractor registry.
fit_notes: Independent minority-owned NC operator with school/SPED service categorization and 20+ year track record; fleet appears very small (~5 employees) so tuck-in scale is marginal but ownership profile (single owner-operator, long tenure) suggests possible succession opportunity.

name: Buck Horn Limousine
recent_news: Self-described as Durango's oldest/largest transportation company (20+ years); ground transport for airport, tours and special events; no evidence of school district or student-transport contracts.
fit_notes: Limo/airport-shuttle operator; not a school bus contractor. Not a fit for Summit's school-bus tuck-in strategy.
